name: Caught in the Web: The KongTuke Intrusion
description:
Background

At 13:00 ZULU, our SOC dashboard lit up with a sequence of troubling alerts originating from the endpoint of employee kthomas. It started with a seemingly harmless web browsing session that was abruptly redirected to a known malicious domain. Within minutes, the EDR agent triggered critical alarms indicating obfuscated PowerShell execution and the silent installation of a portable Python environment.

The Threat

Threat intelligence suggests these are the hallmarks of KongTuke, an advanced threat actor known for leveraging drive-by compromises and custom Python-based implants like ModeloRAT to establish covert command-and-control (C2) channels.

Your Mission

As the incident responder on duty, you have been provided with the consolidated alert logs spanning our Network Proxy, Firewall, and Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) solutions. The logs contain a mix of genuine threats and benign background noise.

You need to cut through the noise, identify the false positives, and reconstruct the complete attack kill chain:

  • Identify the initial infection vector and associated malicious domains.
  • Deobfuscate and analyze the execution chain that led to the payload delivery.
  • Trace the persistence mechanism and the deployment of ModeloRAT.
  • Pinpoint the C2 infrastructure the implant is actively communicating with.

Good luck, hunter. The network depends on you.
